course_subtitle: Welcome to the ORv3 Core Specifications module, where you will delve into the essential features and design elements of the ORv3 rack.
course_description_html: Welcome to the ORv3 Core Specifications module, where you will delve into the essential features and design elements of the ORv3 rack. In this module, you will explore the physical specifications, including dimensions and power shelf configurations, as well as the innovative 48V DC power architecture that enhances energy efficiency. You will also learn about the integrated cooling solutions, both air and liquid, that support high-density workloads, and discover the various accessories and sustainability features that make the ORv3 a versatile choice for modern deployments. Get ready to enhance your understanding of the ORv3 and its capabilities!

blocks.body_html: By the end of this module, you will be able to:

items_html: Identify the physical design specifications of the ORv3 rack, including dimensions, busbar placement, and power shelf configuration
Describe the 48V DC power architecture and its role in improving energy efficiency
Explain how ORv3 supports both air and liquid cooling through integrated airflow and blind-mate manifold design
Explore optional rack accessories and sustainability features that enhance deployment flexibility and environmental impact
Distinguish between support for 21” OCP and legacy 19” EIA equipment within the ORv3 frame
Explain key cabling, mounting, and seismic considerations for ORv3 deployment
Demonstrate how liquid cooling is integrated at the rack level to support high-density workloads

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html:
The ORv3 rack starts with one big shift — it’s designed around a 21-inch equipment bay, wider than the legacy 19” EIA format. This extra width allows for increased airflow, improved power distribution, and more room for cable routing.

ORv3 maintains the standard 42OU rack height and 600mm rack width, but with smart updates inside the frame. The busbar is mounted in the rear center and delivers 48V DC power through blind-mate connections. This design eliminates bulky front-end PDUs and simplifies rack integration.

The power shelf — typically mounted at the top or bottom of the rack — converts incoming 3-phase AC or HVDC to 48V DC and feeds the rear busbar. This centralized delivery model improves power density and simplifies maintenance.

Altogether, ORv3’s structure enhances airflow, cable routing, and power distribution while staying modular and open for a wide range of applications.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html: Let’s explore the 48V DC power architecture that defines ORv3’s electrical design. Discover how power shelves and busbars streamline delivery, reduce energy loss, and improve data center efficiency.
Click on the cards to learn more

back.description_html: A safer, more efficient standard for distributing power across rack equipment.
Reduces current for same power, resulting in smaller conductors, lower heat, and better efficiency.

front.description_html: What is the role of the power shelf?

back.description_html:
Converts incoming AC power to 48V DC and distributes it via the rear-mounted busbar.

https://www.opencompute.org/documents/ocp-spec-v1-0-orv3-power-shelf-universal-input-power-connector-pdf
They allow IT gear to slot in without manual cabling, for faster and safer installation. Learn more here.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html: As compute demands grow, so does the heat. ORv3 is designed to handle it—seamlessly. In this walkthrough, you’ll explore how direct liquid cooling (DLC) is built into the rack itself, enabling higher thermal performance without reengineering your entire setup.
Click the markers on the image to learn more.

items.title: Rear Manifold Integration
items.description_html: Blind-mate manifolds mount directly to the rear frame, enabling hot-swappable connections to DLC-compatible sleds.

items.title: Cold Plate Support
items.description_html: IT gear interfaces with the manifold feeding liquid cooling cold plates using quick-disconnect fittings—no tools required.

items.title: Coolant Loop Flexibility
items.description_html: Supports various cooling topologies (e.g. single-loop, dual-loop), enabling customization for rack-level thermal zones. Such rack-level cooling supports liquid-to-liquid or liquid-to-air heat transfer.

items.title: CDU Compatibility
items.description_html: Racks connect to internal or facility-level CDUs, completing the thermal loop without redesigning rack layout. Shown here is an in-rack CDU. CDU’s may also be external to the rack.

items.title: ASHRAE Alignment
items.description_html: Supports operation at higher thermal setpoints to reduce overall cooling energy, enabling warm-water cooling.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html: Beyond core power and cooling systems, ORv3 includes a range of accessories and structural design choices that enhance its usability and environmental performance. These components improve rack integration, future-proof deployments, and help operators meet efficiency and sustainability targets.
Click on the features to learn more.

items.title: Tool-less features
items.description_html: Reduce install and service time while minimizing technician fatigue and risk.

items.title: Universal Mounting Options
items.description_html: Support for both 21” OCP and legacy 19” EIA IT gear ensures maximum reuse.

items.title: Rear Access Paths
items.description_html: Clean routing for cables and cooling improves operational airflow and serviceability.

items.title: Sidecar Extensions
items.description_html: Optional sidecars expand rack width for additional power or coolant infrastructure without sacrificing footprint efficiency.

items.title: Modular Design
items.description_html: Enables component reuse and flexible expansion as workloads evolve.

items.title: Material Efficiency
items.description_html: Aluminium frame and recyclable parts reduce embedded carbon compared to traditional racks.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html:
ORv3 was designed not just for operation, but also for safe and efficient deployment. In this slide, explore how cabling pathways, lifting brackets, and seismic supports are integrated directly into the rack frame to ensure streamlined setup and long-term durability.
Mounting and moving racks may not be glamorous, but they’re critical to data center efficiency and safety. The ORv3 spec incorporates thoughtful details to ensure cable routing is clean, mounting is secure, and seismic standards are met—supporting both operational ease and regional compliance.
Click on the features to learn more

items.title: Top Cable Channels
items.description_html: Provides clean cable routing across the rack’s top section. Helps reduce airflow disruption and avoid cable congestion.

items.title: Rear Cable Management Bar
items.description_html: A rear bar allows routing of power and signal cables behind the equipment, improving serviceability and reducing strain on connectors.

items.title: Lift & Transport Brackets
items.description_html: Pre-drilled bracket points enable safe lifting and handling during transport or installation, reducing strain and risk of damage.

items.title: Base Tie Downs for Seismic Zones
items.description_html: Anchor points at the base of the rack provide secure attachment to flooring in seismic-prone data centers.

items.title: Adjustable Casters
items.description_html: Heavy-duty casters with locking mechanisms allow the rack to roll into place, then be secured for operation.

items.title: Shock & Vibration Tolerance
items.description_html: Racks are tested to withstand transportation shocks and can be braced to pass zone 4 seismic ratings when installed correctly.

blocks.body_html: Transporting and installing server racks is a major logistical challenge—especially in large-scale data centers. Automated handling solutions are increasingly used to reduce manual effort, minimize risk of damage, and ensure safer, more repeatable installations. Let’s explore tools that work alongside ORv3 racks to streamline movement from dock to deployment.
Click on each product to learn how automated transport systems streamline ORv3 deployment.

items.title: Server Rack Lifter
items.description_html: Automated transport systems like the PHS West Server Rack Lifter streamline ORv3 deployment by enabling precise, low-effort server handling with minimal manual intervention. Designed for OCP form factors, these lifters improve safety and efficiency with pinpoint control, low-clearance access, and customizable stability features.

items.title: Dual Bay Data Server Cart
items.description_html: Automated transport systems like the ACLD-2442-70 streamline ORv3 deployment by safely lifting and maneuvering heavy racks with electric precision, even in tight spaces. With integrated LiDAR sensors, AGV compatibility, and universal support for ORv2 and ORv3 racks, they enhance safety, efficiency, and flexibility during server rack logistics.

items.title: Powered Walkie
items.description_html: Automated transport systems like the powered server rack walkie simplify ORv3 deployment by securely lifting and moving heavy racks with minimal manual effort. Its steel frame, electronic lift, and rack-specific retention features ensure safe, efficient handling throughout the data center.

items.title: Rack Tug
items.description_html: Motorized server rack tugs streamline ORv3 deployment by safely transporting fully loaded, high-power AI racks with precision and control. By reducing ergonomic strain and minimizing handling risks, they accelerate maintenance, upgrades, and integration while protecting sensitive equipment.

items.title: Rack Ramp
items.description_html: Automated transport systems like the PHS West Server Rack Ramp streamline ORv3 deployment by enabling stable, secure loading of heavy racks with minimal manual handling. When paired with motorized tugs, they enhance operational efficiency, protect sensitive equipment, and reduce ergonomic risks for data center staff.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html: Test your knowledge from this module. Select the best answer for each question, then submit.

blocks.question_html: What power delivery upgrade distinguishes ORv3 from earlier Open Rack designs?

choices.text_html: Use of 12V AC to support legacy systems
Switch from 12V DC to 48V DC for higher efficiency
Relocation of power shelf to the front of the rack

blocks.feedback_correct_html: Correct! The 48V DC system reduces current, enabling better energy efficiency and scalability for high-density deployments.
blocks.feedback_incorrect_html: Not quite. The 48V DC system reduces current, enabling better energy efficiency and scalability for high-density deployments.s.

blocks.question_html: What is the primary function of the liquid cooling blind-mate manifold in ORv3 racks?

choices.text_html: Distribute coolant from the remote pumping unit or facility supply into the various IT gear equipment within the rack.

blocks.feedback_correct_html: Correct! ORv3 centralized power with a 12V DC power shelf and busbars to reduce cables and improve serviceability.
blocks.feedback_incorrect_html: Not quite. ORv3 centralized power with a 12V DC power shelf and busbars to reduce cables and improve serviceability.

blocks.question_html: Which of the following features supports ORv3’s commitment to sustainability?

choices.text_html: Higher voltage leads to less current and hence loss due to resistance

blocks.feedback_correct_html: Correct! HPC and AI workloads required higher power density and better cooling, leading to ORv3 development.
blocks.feedback_incorrect_html: Not quite. HPC and AI workloads required higher power density and better cooling, leading to ORv3 development.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html: In this module, you learned how to:

items_html: Identify the key physical specifications of the ORv3 rack, including its dimensions, busbar layout, and power shelf placement
Describe the 48V DC power architecture and how it enhances efficiency across deployments
Explain how airflow and liquid cooling—via blind-mate manifolds—are integrated into the ORv3 design
Explore rack accessories and sustainability features that support modular, energy-conscious deployments
Recognize ORv3’s compatibility with both 21-inch OCP and 19-inch EIA equipment
Explain the rack’s approach to structured cabling, secure mounting, and seismic protection
Demonstrate how liquid cooling systems integrate at the rack level to support high-density compute loads
